message:
fix(channel): add broadcast PolledAfterCompletion guard and preserve watch value with zero receivers

Broadcast channel:
- Recv future now tracks a `completed` flag; polling after Ready
  returns RecvError::PolledAfterCompletion instead of re-entering
  the receive path and potentially double-consuming a message
- New test recv_second_poll_after_ok_fails_closed verifies the guard
- BroadcastStream, channel_conformance, and property_sync_channel
  updated to handle the new error variant (mapped to stream-closed)

Watch channel semantic change:
- Sender::send() and send_modify() no longer fail when receiver_count
  is zero — they store the value so that future subscribers (via
  subscribe()) observe the latest state. The error path is now reserved
  for when the sender itself has been dropped/closed
- This fixes a gap where a watch cell used as a shared-state container
  lost updates during transient zero-receiver windows
- wake_all_waiters() is now skipped when receiver_count == 0 (no one
  to wake), avoiding unnecessary lock acquisition
- Existing test renamed from send_error_when_no_receivers to
  send_without_receivers_preserves_latest_value with updated assertions
- New test send_modify_without_receivers_preserves_latest_value

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
